Qubit.description: The fundamental quantum data type in Q# representing a single quantum bit that exists in a superposition of states. Qubits are allocated using the use statement and cannot be directly copied or inspected.

Result.description: A classical discriminated union type in Q# representing the outcome of a quantum measurement, with two possible values Zero and One corresponding to the computational basis states.

operation.description: Declares a Q# operation that can perform quantum side effects including qubit allocation, gate application, and measurement. Operations are the primary callable type for quantum algorithms.

function.description: Declares a purely classical Q# function with no quantum side effects. Functions cannot allocate qubits, apply gates, or call operations, enabling safe classical computation and optimization.

H.signature: operation H(qubit : Qubit) : Unit is Adj + Ctl
H.description: Applies the Hadamard gate to a single qubit, transforming the basis state |0> into an equal superposition of |0> and |1>. Essential for creating quantum superpositions and interference patterns.

CNOT.signature: operation CNOT(control : Qubit, target : Qubit) : Unit is Adj + Ctl
CNOT.description: Applies the Controlled-NOT gate that flips the target qubit if and only if the control qubit is in state |1>. The fundamental two-qubit gate for creating entanglement in quantum circuits.

M.description: Performs a single-qubit measurement in the computational (Z) basis, collapsing the qubit superposition to either Zero or One and returning the classical Result value.

X.signature: operation X(qubit : Qubit) : Unit is Adj + Ctl
X.description: Applies the Pauli-X gate (quantum NOT) to a single qubit, flipping |0> to |1> and |1> to |0>. Equivalent to a classical bit flip operation on the quantum state.

Z.signature: operation Z(qubit : Qubit) : Unit is Adj + Ctl
Z.description: Applies the Pauli-Z gate to a single qubit, adding a phase of -1 to the |1> component while leaving |0> unchanged. Used for phase kickback and quantum error correction.

Y.signature: operation Y(qubit : Qubit) : Unit is Adj + Ctl
Y.description: Applies the Pauli-Y gate to a single qubit, combining X and Z operations with an imaginary phase factor. Rotates the quantum state around the Y-axis of the Bloch sphere.

Rx.signature: operation Rx(theta : Double, qubit : Qubit) : Unit is Adj + Ctl
Rx.description: Applies a rotation around the X-axis of the Bloch sphere by the specified angle theta in radians. Used for parameterized quantum circuits and variational quantum algorithms.

Rz.signature: operation Rz(theta : Double, qubit : Qubit) : Unit is Adj + Ctl
Rz.description: Applies a rotation around the Z-axis of the Bloch sphere by the specified angle theta in radians. Commonly used for phase manipulation in quantum Fourier transforms.

use.description: Allocates one or more fresh qubits initialized in the |0> state for use within the block scope. Qubits must be returned to |0> state before deallocation at scope exit.

MeasureAllZ.description: Measures all qubits in the given array jointly in the Z-basis and returns a single Result, collapsing the joint quantum state to a computational basis state.

ApplyToEach.description: Applies a single-qubit operation to every element in a qubit register array. A convenience operation for broadcasting gates across multiple qubits uniformly.
